one_liner: TTD -0.26% on 0.50x volume — lowest volume session in months signals supply exhaustion at the VCP base; DCF $33.50 (43% MoS) and dual VCP contractions make this the cleanest base setup in the watchlist.

news_catalyst.headline: No specific news; enterprise SaaS re-rating from July 1 Guggenheim upgrades provides valuation floor
news_catalyst.summary: No new TTD-specific news. The July 1 Guggenheim upgrade of Salesforce and ServiceNow (on valuation — NOT AI bullishness) established a sector floor for the entire beaten-down enterprise SaaS complex. TTD at $19.10 is down 78.7% from its $89.76 August 2025 high — a collapse that the Guggenheim 'SaaSpocalypse overshot' thesis directly addresses. Today's 0.50x volume with a $0.36 price range is the quietest session in months.

technical.summary: TTD's two-contraction VCP is the cleanest base pattern in the watchlist: first contraction from $32.90 (March 5, 2.68x volume peak) fell 36.1% to $21.03 with volume drying; second contraction from $24.32 pulled back only 9.0% to $22.13 with volume also drying. This contracting-range, drying-volume sequence is exactly the VCP setup. Today's 0.50x volume session — essentially no trading — is the coiled-spring moment before the potential expansion. The VCP pivot is $22.13 (second contraction high); a close above it on 1.5x+ volume is the actionable SOS signal. DCF $33.50 vs $19.10 = 43% MoS is the fundamental anchor.

wyckoff_narrative: The VCP within the broader distribution framework creates an unusual setup. The Wyckoff engine labels it Distribution Phase C-D (reflecting the $89 to $17 prior markdown), but at the micro level the two contractions with drying volume show genuine accumulation characteristics at the lows. Today's near-zero volume session is supply exhaustion. The pivot at $22.13 is the actionable level: break on volume = buy signal, violation of $17.47 prior low = stop.

valuation.summary: DCF $33.50 vs $19.10 = 43% MoS — second-largest in the watchlist after NFLX. CTV advertising leadership is intact. Stock collapse was multiple compression, not revenue collapse.

risks: VCP pivot $22.13 must be cleared on volume — currently 16% away, needs a catalyst
0.50x volume = no institutional conviction yet; setup is building, not confirmed
AI disruption to traditional programmatic advertising DSPs is a real long-term risk
